topic: Minimax-optimal heterogeneous Bernoulli design for bipartite-interference experiments: minimize a graph-only worst-case design-based variance envelope over p subject to a budget and positivity floor, with an observable approximation certificate, non-identical-Bernoulli Hajek CLT, and conservative Wald-valid variance estimator extending arXiv:2501.09844.

open_problems.open_problem: Derive the closed-form graph-only worst-case Hajek variance envelope for heterogeneous Bernoulli bipartite experiments and solve the budgeted positivity-constrained minimization over p.

source_refs.claim: The paper proves design-based bipartite Hajek consistency, CLT, and conservative variance for a single Bernoulli(p) assignment probability, leaving richer bipartite design mechanisms outside its proved theory.
source_refs.author: Sizhu Lu, Lei Shi, Yue Fang, Wenxin Zhang, Peng Ding

source_refs.claim: The paper treats bipartite design as constrained by the bipartite graph and introduces Exposure-Design heuristics under a linear exposure-response model, not a model-free bounded-potential-outcome minimax envelope.
source_refs.author: Christopher Harshaw, Fredrik Savje, David Eisenstat, Vahab Mirrokni, Jean Pouget-Abadie

source_refs.claim: The paper optimizes network experiment design to minimize estimator variance using pilot-estimated quantities in a one-mode two-wave framework, rather than a graph-only bipartite minimax envelope.
source_refs.author: Davide Viviano

source_refs.excerpt: Open: characterize when a feasible +-1 design ATTAINS the relaxation optimum.

open_problems.why_unsolved: The homogeneous Lu-Shi-Fang-Zhang-Ding variance matrices become p-dependent through products over shared treatment-unit neighborhoods once assignments have non-identical probabilities. Taking the supremum over bounded potential outcomes turns the problem into a graph-functional optimization with reciprocal product weights, random Hajek denominators, a global budget constraint, and a positivity floor; prior CausalSmith design-certificate work shows analogous relaxation exactness questions remain open even for simpler +-1 covariance implementability objectives.
open_problems.what_a_resolution_would_look_like: A theorem defining V_env(G,p)=sup_{|Y_i(z)|<=1} AVAR_G,p(hat_tau_Hajek;Y) in closed form and proving that a computable p_star(G) minimizes V_env(G,p) over p in [epsilon,1-epsilon]^m with sum_k p_k=B, with KKT or dual optimality conditions.

writing_exemplar.why: It is the direct motivation arc: design-based bipartite inference under weak outcome assumptions, homogeneous Bernoulli analysis, then the missing heterogeneous design layer.
writing_exemplar.motivation_arc: model-based bipartite methods need strong assumptions -> homogeneous design-based Hajek inference avoids outcome modeling -> heterogeneous p should be optimized for graph-only minimax precision

method_exemplars.technique: Variance-minimizing network experimental design with inference and regret analysis; adapt the proof separation between design selection and subsequent treatment-effect inference.
method_exemplars.why_closest: It solves a structurally analogous design-for-precision problem under network interference, while the target replaces pilot-estimated variance by a graph-only worst-case Hajek envelope.

method_exemplars.technique: Bipartite exposure-probability algebra and design-aware variance control for an estimator on a sparse bipartite graph.
method_exemplars.why_closest: It is bipartite and design-aware, so its graph/exposure bookkeeping is the closest analogue even though its linear exposure-response model must be replaced by bounded potential outcomes.

open_problems.open_problem: Prove an observable convex-relaxation-plus-rounding approximation certificate for the heterogeneous Bernoulli minimax design, with a nonvacuous ratio under an explicit output-degree-dispersion regime.

source_refs.claim: The paper shows graph-aware randomization can avoid network-exposure variance blowups under structural graph regimes, motivating certificates that state when a graph makes a design tractable.
source_refs.author: Johan Ugander, Brian Karrer, Lars Backstrom, Jon Kleinberg

source_refs.claim: The paper replaces fixed cluster-randomization variance pathologies by randomized graph cluster designs with polynomial-style variance control under graph metric assumptions.
source_refs.author: Johan Ugander, Hao Yin

source_refs.claim: The paper selects clustered designs using design-dependent bias and variance bounds, but in low-order outcome models rather than arbitrary bounded potential outcomes.
source_refs.author: Matthew Eichhorn, Samir Khan, Johan Ugander, Christina Lee Yu

source_refs.excerpt: Gaussian (Goemans-Williamson) rounding gives only a ONE-SIDED approximation-ratio certificate.
rounding-loss certificate rho* bounding the price of +-1 implementability

open_problems.why_unsolved: Existing graph-design guarantees are mostly for cluster designs, model-based exposure estimators, or SDP rounding objectives whose feasible randomized designs have a different geometry. The heterogeneous Bernoulli objective has continuous probabilities, reciprocal product inclusion weights, and a worst-case potential-outcome envelope; a useful certificate must be computable from G, B, epsilon, and observable degree summaries rather than from unknown outcomes.
open_problems.what_a_resolution_would_look_like: A polynomial-time relaxation and rounding theorem returning p_hat and C(G,p_hat) such that V_env(G,p_hat) <= alpha(G) min_p V_env(G,p), plus a proof that alpha(G) <= alpha_0 under an explicit dispersion condition such as max_k d_k^2/sum_l d_l^2 <= c for intervention-unit output degrees.

writing_exemplar.why: Its framing cleanly turns graph structure into a variance-control question, which is the same narrative needed for a nonvacuous approximation certificate.
writing_exemplar.motivation_arc: naive randomization can have graph-driven variance blowup -> graph regimes restore provable control -> heterogeneous Bernoulli rounding needs an observable graph regime

method_exemplars.technique: Convex programming over conservative quadratic variance upper bounds, with proof that the optimized bound remains design-based conservative.
method_exemplars.why_closest: The target also optimizes a conservative design-based variance functional, but over heterogeneous Bernoulli probabilities and a bipartite Hajek envelope.

method_exemplars.technique: Graph/design-dependent bias-variance bounds used to select among experimental designs, with observable design-comparison criteria.
method_exemplars.why_closest: It supplies a design-selection certificate template; the target replaces low-order outcome models by worst-case bounded potential outcomes.

open_problems.open_problem: Establish a non-identical-Bernoulli Hajek martingale CLT and closed-form asymptotic variance for bipartite all-treated/all-control exposure contrasts under graph sparsity and optimized p.

source_refs.claim: The anchor paper proves a bipartite Hajek CLT and closed-form asymptotic variance for homogeneous Bernoulli randomization under sparse bipartite graph conditions.
source_refs.author: Sizhu Lu, Lei Shi, Yue Fang, Wenxin Zhang, Peng Ding

source_refs.claim: The paper allows independent but not necessarily identical treatment probabilities and proves asymptotic normality under approximate neighborhood interference, but not for bipartite all-treated/all-control product exposures.
source_refs.author: Michael P. Leung

source_refs.claim: The paper gives the general exposure-mapping/IPW framework with joint exposure probabilities, conservative variance, and local-dependence asymptotics, but not the optimized heterogeneous bipartite Hajek array.
source_refs.author: Peter M. Aronow, Cyrus Samii

source_refs.excerpt: The advertised CLT is therefore not a new limit theorem or sharper rate
feasible conservative Wald under growing degree Delta_n=o(n^(1/4))

open_problems.why_unsolved: The exposure indicators for all-treated and all-control bipartite units are products over outcome neighborhoods. With heterogeneous p_k, first and joint inclusion probabilities vary sharply across outcome units; the Hajek ratio expansion, martingale difference construction, Lindeberg condition, and variance nondegeneracy checks must be rebuilt for a non-i.i.d. triangular array. Prior in-repo CLT work was downgraded because it only routed exposure bookkeeping into an existing dependency-graph theorem rather than proving a new Hajek-specific limit theorem or sharper rate.
open_problems.what_a_resolution_would_look_like: A theorem proving a studentizable CLT for the centered heterogeneous-p Hajek estimator with variance v_{G,p}(Y)=n^{-2}{Y_1' Lambda_1(p)Y_1+Y_0' Lambda_0(p)Y_0+2Y_1' Lambda_10(p)Y_0}, explicit Lambda matrices from joint bipartite exposure probabilities, and graph/p nondegeneracy and sparsity assumptions that allow optimized p.

writing_exemplar.why: It supplies the exact homogeneous proof target and notation the heterogeneous-p CLT must generalize.
writing_exemplar.motivation_arc: homogeneous Bernoulli product exposures give a bipartite Hajek CLT -> heterogeneous p changes overlap weights and denominator behavior -> non-identical-Bernoulli martingale CLT is needed

method_exemplars.technique: Network-interference asymptotic normality with independent non-identical treatment probabilities and graph dependence controls.
method_exemplars.why_closest: It shows non-identical Bernoulli assignment can be compatible with network causal CLTs, but the target must adapt the machinery to product exposures and Hajek denominators.

method_exemplars.technique: Exposure and joint-exposure probability calculus plus conservative design-based variance under interference.
method_exemplars.why_closest: The heterogeneous bipartite variance matrices are built from the same exposure-probability primitives.

open_problems.open_problem: Construct a conservative variance estimator and Wald-coverage theorem that remain valid after p is chosen by a graph-only minimax design rule.

source_refs.claim: The anchor paper proves conservative variance estimation and Wald inference for the homogeneous bipartite Bernoulli Hajek estimator.
source_refs.author: Sizhu Lu, Lei Shi, Yue Fang, Wenxin Zhang, Peng Ding

source_refs.claim: The paper frames conservative variance estimation under interference as fragmented across designs, models, and estimators, motivating general recomputation-based conservative inference.
source_refs.author: Bryan Park, Stefan Wager

source_refs.claim: The paper warns that convenient network-robust standard errors can be anti-conservative under interference and develops adjusted covariance estimators to improve coverage.
source_refs.author: Mengsi Gao, Peng Ding

source_refs.claim: The paper formulates minimally conservative variance estimation under interference as an optimization over estimable conservative quadratic upper bounds.
source_refs.author: Christopher Harshaw, Joel A. Middleton, Fredrik Savje

source_refs.excerpt: Vbar_n is a completion supremum, not minimal over quadratic-form variance estimators

open_problems.why_unsolved: The true heterogeneous-p variance contains cross-potential-outcome terms that remain unobservable, and optimizing p can push inclusion probabilities toward the positivity floor where variance-estimator instability is most likely. The proof must separate graph-based design choice from assignment randomness, show conservativeness for the selected p_hat rather than a fixed p, and avoid the in-repo pitfall where a completion supremum is tautologically sharp only within its own envelope class but not estimator-class optimal or practically tight.
open_problems.what_a_resolution_would_look_like: A computable estimator V_hat(G,p_hat,Z,Y_obs) with E_Z[V_hat|G,p_hat] >= v_{G,p_hat}(Y) for all bounded potential outcomes, plus a theorem that the Wald interval hat_tau +/- z_{1-alpha/2} sqrt(V_hat) has asymptotic coverage at least 1-alpha uniformly over the graph and p_hat regime used by the minimax certificate.

writing_exemplar.why: It motivates conservative randomization inference under interference as a design-based necessity when exact variance components are unobserved.
writing_exemplar.motivation_arc: interference makes exact variance hard to estimate -> conservative variance is the design-based fallback -> optimized heterogeneous p needs post-design Wald validity

method_exemplars.technique: Closed-form conservative variance estimator and Wald theorem for homogeneous bipartite Hajek inference.
method_exemplars.why_closest: It is the exact inferential statement to generalize from scalar p to graph-optimized heterogeneous p.

method_exemplars.technique: Estimator-class optimization of conservative quadratic variance upper bounds under interference.
method_exemplars.why_closest: It addresses the same obstacle named by the prior proposal: proving more than completion-supremum tautological sharpness.

literature_map:
Lu, Shi, Fang, Zhang, and Ding 2025 are the direct anchor: design-based bipartite Hajek inference, homogeneous Bernoulli(p), sparse graph CLT, conservative variance, and covariate adjustment under weak outcome assumptions.
Zigler and Papadogeorgou 2021 and Doudchenko et al. 2020 establish bipartite causal setups and GPS-style inference, but rely on simplified estimands or modeling/propensity assumptions rather than graph-only minimax design.
Harshaw, Savje, Eisenstat, Mirrokni, and Pouget-Abadie 2021 study bipartite design and estimation under a linear exposure-response model; precision is design-aware but model-based.
Shi et al. 2024 make bipartite analysis scalable at platform scale and show practical CI behavior depends on graph density and estimator choice.
Aronow and Samii 2017 provide the general exposure-mapping/IPW framework with joint exposure probabilities and conservative randomization variance.
Leung 2019 shows independent non-identical treatment probabilities can be handled in network-interference CLTs, suggesting but not proving the heterogeneous bipartite Hajek extension.
Ugander et al. 2013 and Ugander-Yin 2020 show graph-aware designs can avoid exposure-variance blowups under explicit graph regimes.
Viviano 2026 optimizes network experiment design for variance and inference using pilot data, contrasting with the desired graph-only worst-case minimax objective.
Eichhorn et al. 2024 use design-dependent bias and variance bounds to select clustered designs, but under low-order outcome models.
Harshaw, Middleton, and Savje 2021 optimize conservative variance bounds via convex programming, a natural template for estimator-class certificates.
Park and Wager 2026 emphasize that conservative variance estimation under interference is fragmented by design/model/estimator, motivating general conservative frameworks.
Gao and Ding 2023 warn that convenient network-robust SEs can be anti-conservative under interference.
The key tension is design optimality versus model agnosticism: graph-aware designs often gain precision by modeling exposure/outcomes, while the Lu et al. design-based framework avoids outcome modeling but does not yet optimize heterogeneous assignment probabilities.
prior_proposal_map:
Mined exp_bipartite_minimax_design state/pipeline: only init/state existed, no proposal text yet.
Mined exp_design_pm1_exactness_boundary_v1 README/reviews for design relaxations, rounding loss, and exactness/frontier certificates; this cross-linked to the heterogeneous p minimax and approximation-certificate gaps.
Mined exp_rollout_chebyshev_minimax reviews/state for exact-covariance and estimator/CLT follow-on gaps; useful adjacent design-covariance substrate but not emitted after G1-G5 filtering for this topic.
Mined exp_ego_cluster_exposure_clt README/pipeline for design-based CLT, conservative Wald, and variance-envelope optimality gaps; this cross-linked to the non-identical-Bernoulli CLT and post-design variance-estimator gaps.
Mined exp_saturation_skew_threshold README for heterogeneous-design re-raise after homogeneous V3=V4 degeneracy; adjacent but not emitted because it is saturation-design rather than bipartite Hajek minimax.
Mined pid_network_exposure proposal/reviews for shared graph-completion compatibility and matching/cover certificate conjectures; adjacent partial-ID substrate, not emitted.
Mined pid_network_random_set angle-rejected proposal for sparse Bernoulli-law collision quotient/testing frontier; adjacent, not emitted.
Mined panel_approx_nbhd_interference, pid_network_exposure_aliasing_graphpair, and eid_market_matching readmes/reviews for adjacent inference, Bernoulli exposure, and bipartite certificate candidates.
